user_query:
I'm tracking wrist and eye strain from long drawing sessions. Please analyze my 14-day health/work log and update my ergonomics plan, then draft a concise email to my editor about the changes.

Inputs:
- input/work_log.csv (daily entries for 2026-03-01 through 2026-03-14)
- docs/ErgonomicsRoutine.md (my current ergonomics plan with placeholder items)

Deliverables and requirements:
1) Create a data-driven summary report at reports/health_summary.md that includes:
   - The date range covered and the total number of days analyzed.
   - Per-week averages (Week 1 = Mar 1–7, Week 2 = Mar 8–14), rounded to 1 decimal place, for: hours_drawing, breaks, neck_pain, wrist_pain, eye_strain, and sleep_hours.
   - A list of wrist pain flare days defined as wrist_pain >= 7. For each flare day, list the date and pages_drawn.
   - The Week 1 vs Week 2 wrist_pain difference (to 1 decimal) and a conclusion line exactly following this rule:
     • If (Week 2 avg wrist_pain – Week 1 avg wrist_pain) >= 0.5, include the line: "Wrist pain trend: WORSENED".
     • Otherwise include: "Wrist pain trend: STABLE/IMPROVED".

2) Edit docs/ErgonomicsRoutine.md in place (do not create a new file) to replace the "Planned Changes (Draft)" checklist with exactly three data-informed items:
   - Min breaks/day: B_target = ceil(Week 2 average breaks per day) + 1
   - Max drawing hours/day: H_target = (Week 2 average hours_drawing – 0.5), rounded down to the nearest 0.5
   - Stop-work rule: "If wrist pain >= 7, stop drawing for the day and schedule recovery."
   Also, under the "Pain Points" section, add a short "Data Snapshot (Mar 1–14)" subsection listing the top two symptoms by Week 2 average severity among neck_pain, wrist_pain, and eye_strain (use their names and Week 2 averages rounded to 1 decimal). Remove all placeholder lines in the checklist.

3) Draft an email at outbox/email_to_editor.txt with:
   - First line as: "Subject: Ergonomic plan update and schedule request (Mar 1–14)"
   - A brief body that:
     • Summarizes whether wrist pain worsened or not using the same wording as the report ("WORSENED" or "STABLE/IMPROVED").
     • States the two numeric targets you set (Min breaks/day = B_target and Max drawing hours/day = H_target).
     • Proposes: "reduce daily pages by 1 on flare days" and references the flare-day definition (wrist_pain >= 7).
     • Mentions both local artifacts by path: reports/health_summary.md and docs/ErgonomicsRoutine.md.

Notes:
- Use only the provided local files. Do not rename paths.
- Ensure numeric values in docs/ErgonomicsRoutine.md and the email match the computations in reports/health_summary.md. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
